message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My feature (Translation Metadata Tracking for trans tag) requires editing src/jinja2/ext.py (parse() and _make_node()). Please let me know which files your feature touches so we can find a disjoint split.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain param support to trans tag in ext.py InternationalizationExtension

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both features require ext.py (parse()/_make_node()). Since a disjoint split isn't possible here, let's each implement independently on our own branches now. When merging, I'll resolve conflicts by combining both changes (domain param + trans_metadata dict) rather than dropping either. I'll keep my diff to metadata-specific additions (parsing note/created/modified, building metadata dict, setting trans_metadata attr) and avoid touching unrelated parts of parse()/_make_node() signatures where possible.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both touch parse()/_make_node() in InternationalizationExtension - can't split by file. Proposal: I'll implement domain support now (small, additive change: new domain= kwarg parsing branch, and dgettext/dngettext lookup in _make_node), commit and push to agent1 branch quickly. Then you pull/merge agent1 into your branch and layer your metadata tracking on top so there's no conflict. I'll do the final required merge of team/agent2 at the end regardless. OK with this order, or would you rather go first?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first with domain support, push to agent1 branch. I'll wait, then fetch+merge team/agent1 into my branch and layer trans_metadata on top. Ping me (or I'll poll) when your DONE is sent.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - implementing independently now, will carefully merge both changes (domain param + trans_metadata) into _make_node signature/parse loop when I do the final required merge. Will keep my changes minimal/additive to reduce conflict surface.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Metadata tracking (note/created/modified) implemented and tested (830 tests pass, all required syntax patterns + error cases verified). Ready whenever you want to do the final merge, or let me know if you'd like me to merge team/agent1 into my branch instead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain support implemented, merged team/agent2 (metadata tracking) into agent1 - resolved 4 conflicts in parse()/_make_node() to combine both features (domain + metadata dicts, comma handling, signature ordering with domain/metadata both defaulting to None/{}). All 830 tests pass, verified domain+metadata+variables combos work together. Pushed to team/agent1.
